summary: FQ-10 adds six depth-banded ore families (coal, copper_ore, tin_ore at tier-1 pick; iron_ore, silver_ore, crystal behind the tier-2 gate) as data. Generation is data-defined via a new world_settings.json ore_table (per-family min/max depth, frequency, threshold, unique seed_offset): WorldGen._build_ore_families makes one FastNoiseLite per family (seed = world_seed + seed_offset) and _ore_family_at returns the first family whose band contains the cell and whose channel clears its threshold, else stone. Families claim only cells that would be stone - the generic 'ore' vein decision runs first and is byte-identical, so every prior ore check passes unchanged. ore_abundance scales all thresholds and 0 disables every ore. Each ore drops itself, is pick-preferred, and has no recipe consumer yet (FQ-11 furnace/ingots) - 'avoid making every ore immediately useful' respected. Thresholds were calibrated to FastNoiseLite's real output range (0.58-0.72) after the smoke caught a first pass at 0.78-0.87 producing near-zero deeper ore. Distinct BLOCK_COLORS and items.json swatches render the ores until block art lands. Suite 257 -> 262 (5 fq10_* checks); validator enforces the ore blocks and the ore_table contract; capsule doctor healthy; no save-schema change.
scope: FQ-10 only per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md - ore-family data and depth-banded generation. No new stations, recipes, gameplay consumers, save-schema change, or UI beyond fallback colors/icons.
next_action: FQ-11: workbench/furnace/anvil station chain - the first consumer for the new ores (smelting, ingots). Block art for the ores via docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md.
